Hi,


It connects but disconnects in a few seconds. Can you help me? Thank you.

---

### Post by ModelM on 2009-02-24
This is a very unusual login sequence. You might try adding the line:

Stupid Mode = 1

to your /etc/wvdial.conf file. This command will start ppp immediately upon connection & do the login through pap or chap. Some services are set up so that if you want a ppp connection you begin ppp right away, and if you want a text-based connection, you continue with the text login. This might be one of those.

You'll need to be root for pap/chap to work so the command would be

sudo wvdial

...hope this helps

---

### Post by ModelM on 2009-02-27
OK, remove the "Stupid Mode" line, since it didn't work.

The first login sequence you posted shows a successful login. The problem is that the ISP doesn't start PPP and wvdial cannot figure out how to tell the ISP to start PPP.

I'd call the ISP & ask them how to initiate a PPP session.

---
